sections.question_synthetic: What disclosure requirements apply to filers with interests in limited partnerships, and when must general partners be reported on Statement of Economic Interests forms?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Filers must report limited partnership interests valued at $1,000 or more on Schedule A-Investments. General partners must be reported only if there are no more than 35 partners, no more than two controlling partners, and the filer has an interest of $2,000 or more. Controlling general partners should be listed underneath the limited partnership name in parentheses on Schedule A.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a memorandum to FPPC staff rather than a traditional advice letter format. Significant OCR degradation affects text clarity throughout. Q/C sections appear to follow Q&A format embedded in body text rather than formal sections. Conclusion synthesized from multiple staff Q&A exchanges. Document references external opinion (Nord Opinion 83-004) for detailed authority.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What disclosure requirements apply to filers with interests in limited partnerships, and when must general partners be reported on Statement of Economic Interests forms?

CONCLUSION: Filers must report limited partnership interests valued at $1,000 or more on Schedule A-Investments. General partners must be reported only if there are no more than 35 partners, no more than two controlling partners, and the filer has an interest of $2,000 or more. Controlling general partners should be listed underneath the limited partnership name in parentheses on Schedule A.

embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum providing guidance to staff on disclosure requirements for limited partnership interests and the reporting of general partners under FPPC regulations, referencing Nord Opinion No. 83-004.
